name: Jiao He
page_title: Jiao He 焦和 [Han] – Romance of the Three Kingdoms Encyclopedia – Kongming’s Archives

sections.short-biography: Intellectual, led troops against Dong Zhuo but allowed Turbans to take over.
sections.details:
Traditional Chinese 
	焦和


	 Pronunciation 
	Jiao1 He2


	 Pinyin (Tones) 
	Jiāo Hé


	 Wade-Giles 
	Chiao Ho
sections.biography-source-undefined:
There is nothing known about his origins. Jiao He was the Inspector of Qing province during the Chuping era. He affiliated himself with the Guandong coalition. He tried to enter the regions around Luoyang, but had no time to save its people because of the Yellow Turbans, so he went north across the Yellow river and went back.

Jiao He was a very incompetent leader. As the Yellow Turbans in Qing province grew in numbers, they devastated the cities. Jiao He, despite having many good quality weapons and a lot of soldiers, failed to give resistance to them. He was very superstitious as well. He often talked to those that divined by yarrow to get their predictions and always had sorcerers that prayed for luck or astronomers by his side. He declined outside assistance and refused to act military against the Yellow Turbans, claiming that assumptions would be made against him if he acted; he instead vainly wishing that they would leave. It is said that in winter, when the Yellow river froze, Jiao He ordered the throwing of ice-breaking objects to smash the river’s ice and thus prevent the Turbans from crossing. Within the governmental offices, Jiao He debated with scholars and talked about ambitions but he effectively did nothing outside of his offices and his orders failed to even get to their destination. His administration was so negligent that there was disorder everywhere in Qing province. Jiao He would eventually die of illness around 194. After Jiao He’s death, Yuan Shao sent Zang Hong to replace Jiao He as Inspector of Qing province.

full_text:
Jiao He 焦和
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
Jiao He (焦和). Intellectual, led troops against Dong Zhuo but allowed Turbans to take over. Jiao He was affiliated with the Han Dynasty. Jiao He lived during the Later Han Dynasty period of Chinese history.

BiographySource Undefined
There is nothing known about his origins. Jiao He was the Inspector of Qing province during the Chuping era. He affiliated himself with the Guandong coalition. He tried to enter the regions around Luoyang, but had no time to save its people because of the Yellow Turbans, so he went north across the Yellow river and went back.
Jiao He was a very incompetent leader. As the Yellow Turbans in Qing province grew in numbers, they devastated the cities. Jiao He, despite having many good quality weapons and a lot of soldiers, failed to give resistance to them. He was very superstitious as well. He often talked to those that divined by yarrow to get their predictions and always had sorcerers that prayed for luck or astronomers by his side. He declined outside assistance and refused to act military against the Yellow Turbans, claiming that assumptions would be made against him if he acted; he instead vainly wishing that they would leave. It is said that in winter, when the Yellow river froze, Jiao He ordered the throwing of ice-breaking objects to smash the river’s ice and thus prevent the Turbans from crossing. Within the governmental offices, Jiao He debated with scholars and talked about ambitions but he effectively did nothing outside of his offices and his orders failed to even get to their destination. His administration was so negligent that there was disorder everywhere in Qing province. Jiao He would eventually die of illness around 194. After Jiao He’s death, Yuan Shao sent Zang Hong to replace Jiao He as Inspector of Qing province.
